title: theodorea schultzii
intro: Theodorea schultzii, commonly known as the 'Corkwood Tree', is a plant species in the family Hernandiaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from various health issues. Theodorea schultzii is believed to have several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help in the treatment of wounds, fever, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents present in this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic actions, such as analgesic, anti-oxidant, and anti-inflammatory effects.
side_effects: Theodorea schultzii can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, dizziness, and allergic reactions in some individuals. In rare cases, excessive consumption can lead to vomiting, diarrhea, and abdominal pain. Additionally, improper use of the plant can also cause skin irritation, including redness, itching, and rashes in sensitive individuals. Prolonged exposure to the plant's essential oils may also trigger respiratory issues, such as congestion, coughing, and shortness of breath.
